concept: Baseline / Stated Reference Condition for Comparison
description: A stated reference condition chosen for comparison, which can be historical, modeled, or administratively set. A baseline need not be the earliest, healthiest, or most natural state a place has held; it is simply the point everything else gets measured against.
sound_symbolism: The complete foundation-bearing form 'motha' sets a reference beneath later comparisons without pretending it is the beginning of the world. Final 'mi' gathers the selected state, measurements, or period into one held comparison point that can still be revised.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'ha mirela ha mothami senao ma nai.' (This state is not the same as this baseline.) State variables, place, period, method, source, and uncertainty where relevant. Distinct from 'motha' (foundation), `mirela` (state), target condition, ordinary past, and an exact reference dataset.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Baselines make restoration and monitoring claims accountable while preventing a preferred past or selected dataset from becoming an unquestioned natural ideal.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The reference is acknowledged as selected, conditioned, and revisable rather than an eternal original state.

tags.cognition: a selected reference condition used for comparison
tags.temporal: often a documented earlier period or state against which change is assessed
